howItWorks.intro: This investment return calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Total Return = Final Value - Initial Investment
howItWorks.outro: Tax laws and financial markets change frequently. Verify current rates with your financial institution.

faq.question: What is CAGR?
faq.answer: Compound Annual Growth Rate shows the average annual return assuming reinvestment. A $10,000 investment growing to $25,000 in 5 years has a CAGR of 20.1%.

faq.question: Is CAGR the same as average return?
faq.answer: No. CAGR accounts for compounding and is more accurate. Simple average return can be misleading when returns vary year to year.

faq.question: What is a good CAGR?
faq.answer: S&P 500 historical CAGR is about 10%. Beating 10% consistently is excellent. Even 7-8% after inflation is strong long-term performance.

faq.question: Does this account for fees?
faq.answer: This uses your actual starting and ending values. If fees reduced your balance, they are already reflected in the calculation.

faq.question: Is the Investment Return Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

sections.title: What Is the Investment Return Calculator?
sections.body: The Investment Return Calculator is a free online tool designed for individuals and families who need quick, accurate calculations in the financial planning space. By entering your initial investment, current value, years held, you get instant results including total return, total return, annualized return. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting total return right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In financial planning, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by individuals and families worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to manage your finances with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your initial investment and need to find the right total return. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Initial Investment ($)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your initial investment in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Current Value ($)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your current value in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Years Held
howToSteps.description: Type or select your years held in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your total return, total return, annualized return. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your initial investment before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both total return and total return — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to manage your finances.
If you're unsure about your years held, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
